dtcs.description: Gauge Control Module Lost Communication With the TPMS Control Unit (TPMS Message) for TPMS indicator does not go off, and no DTCs are stored See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Testing and Inspection/Symptom Related Diagnostic Procedures/TPMS Indicator Does Not Go Off, And No DTCs Are Stored .

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC U0127 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections between the gauge control module   and the TPMS control unit.
Check for TPMS DTCs with the HDS. Are any DTCs indicated? Go to the TPMS   indicated DTCs troubleshooting, then recheck. Go to step 6.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.
Disconnect the TPMS control unit 20P connector.

Check for continuity between the gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 13   and No. 14   and TPMS control unit 20P connector terminals No. 11   and No. 2   respectively. Is there continuity? Substitute a known-good TPMS control unit  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Tire Monitoring System/Tire Pressure Module/Service and Repair, and recheck. If the indication goes away, replace the original TPMS control unit. If the DTC is still present, replace the gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair. Repair an open or poor connection in the wire between the TPMS control unit   and the gauge control module.

dtcs.description: Gauge Control Module Lost Communication With Power Control Unit

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, and ON mode.
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC U128B indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections between the power control unit   and the gauge control module.
From the BODY ELECTRICAL SYSTEM SELECT menu, select B-CAN CONTROL UNITS INFORMATION, and then select CHECK CONNECTED CONTROL UNITS. Is the power control unit detected? Go to step 9. Go to step 6.
Measure the voltage between body ground and power control unit connector C (36P) terminal No. 1. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 8. Go to step 7.

Check the No. 15 (10 A) fuse in the under-hood fuse/relay box. Is the fuse OK? Repair an open or high resistance in the No. 15(10 A)   fuse circuit. Replace the fuse. If the fuse blows again, repair a short to ground in the No. 15(10 A)   fuse circuit.

Measure the voltage between body ground and power control unit connector C (36P) terminals No. 18 and No. 36 individually. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to step 9. Repair an open or high resistance in the ground wire or poor ground (G402).
Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect power control unit connector C (36P).
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 29   and No. 30   and power control unit connector C (36P) terminals No. 17   and No. 16   respectively. Is there continuity? Replace the power control unit. Repair an open or poor connection in the B-CAN wire between the power control unit   and the gauge control module.

steps.instruction: Connect the HDS to the DLC  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/How to Troubleshoot the AcuraLink.
Turn the ignition switch to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the ON mode.

Check for DTCs with the HDS and DTC log data in the navigation system diagnostic mode  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Testing and Inspection/Initial Inspection and Diagnostic Overview/AcuraLink Self-Diagnostic Function. Is DTC U128D indicated with HDS and on the navigation display? Go to step 4. Intermittent failure, the B-CAN communication line is OK at this time. Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the AcuraLink control unit (XM receiver).
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect AcuraLink control unit (XM receiver) connector A (32P) and the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between AcuraLink control unit (XM receiver) connector A (32P) terminals No. 8 and No. 9 and gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 29 and No. 30 respectively. Is there continuity? Check for poor connections or loose terminals at the AcuraLink control unit (XM receiver). If OK, Update the AcuraLink control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning   if it does not have the latest software. If the software is the latest, replace the AcuraLink control unit (XM receiver)  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Radio, Stereo, and Compact Disc/Testing and Inspection/Programming and Relearning. Repair an open in the wire between the AcuraLink control unit (XM receiver)   and the gauge control module.

dtcs.description: Gauge Control Module Lost Communication With BSI Control Unit (BSI Message) The controller area network (CAN) transmits/receives pulsing signals to/from the control modules simultaneously by using two signal lines (CANH and CANL). When the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) does not receive the signals from the EPS control unit via the F-CAN lines for a set time, the ECM/PCM detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: The ECM/PCM does not receive any signals via the F-CAN lines for at least 1 second.
When a malfunction is detected, a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the ECM/PCM memory. The MIL does not come
The Pending DTC, the Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.

dtcs.description: Gauge Control Module Lost Communication With SRS Unit (SRS Message) s/SRS Indicator Stays On, But No DTCs Are Stored, Or Cannot Be Read/Symptom Troubleshooting. The controller area network (CAN) transmits/receives pulsing signals to/from the control modules simultaneously by using two signal lines (CANH and CANL). When the information is not sent from the gauge control module via the F-CAN lines and this condition continues for a specified time or when the information sent from the gauge control module is abnormal and this condition continues for a specified cycle, the engine control module (ECM)/powertrain control module (PCM) detects a malfunction and stores a DTC.
detection_condition: One of these conditions must be met:
The ECM/PCM does not receive any signals via the F-CAN lines for at least 1 second.
The information sent from the gauge control module is abnormal at least 20 times.
When a malfunction is detected, the MIL comes on and a Pending DTC, a Confirmed DTC, and the freeze data are stored in the ECM/PCM memory.
The MIL is cleared if the malfunction does not return in three consecutive trips in which the diagnostic runs. The MIL, the Pending DTC, the Confirmed
DTC, and the freeze data can be cleared with the scan tool Clear command or by disconnecting the battery.

dtcs.description: Invalid Data Received from PCM (Vehicle Speed Data)

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Start the engine, then wait at least 3 seconds.
Test-drive the vehicle at 6 mph (10 km/h) or more.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC U0423 indicated? Troubleshoot the indicated PGM-FI system DTC(s).   After troubleshooting, clear the DTC. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. AcuraLink Control Unit U1280, U1280, U1280, U1280, U1280, U1280, U1280, U1280, U1280, U1280, U1280 DTC U1280: Communication Bus Line Error (BUS-OFF)
Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0) and then back to ON (II), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode and then the ON mode.
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC U1280 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections, or worn/shorted wires. If the connections are good, check the battery condition  See: Starting and Charging/Battery/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Battery Test   and the charging system.
Turn the ignition switch to LOCK (0), or press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.

Disconnect the appropriate connector at each control unit in the table one at a time. Clear the DTC, then recheck for DTCs after each unit is disconnected. Is DTC U1280 indicated with each individual unit disconnected? Leave the connectors disconnected, and go to step 7. Go to the input test (or circuit troubleshooting) for the control unit that was disconnected when DTC U1280 did not reset, and perform all power and ground input tests. dtcs.description: Gauge Control Module Lost Communication With Keyless Access Control Unit

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, and ON mode.
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC U1286 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections between the gauge control module   and the keyless access control unit.

From the BODY ELECTRICAL SYSTEM SELECT menu, select B-CAN CONTROL UNITS INFORMATION, and then select CHECK CONNECTED CONTROL UNITS. Is the keyless access control unit detected? Go to step 9. Go to step 6.
Measure the voltage between body ground and keyless access control unit connector B (32P) terminals No. 1 and No. 17 individually. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 8. Go to step 7.

Check the No. 15 (10 A) fuse in the under-hood fuse/relay box and the No. 9 (20 A) fuse in the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box. Are the fuses OK? Repair an open or high resistance in the No. 15 (10 A) and/or No. 9 (20 A) fuse circuit. Replace the fuse(s). If the fuse blows again, repair a short to ground in the No. 15 (10 A) and/or No. 9 (20 A)   fuse circuit.

Measure the voltage between body ground and keyless access control unit connector A (36P) and B (32P) terminals as shown. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to step 9. Repair an open or high resistance in the ground wire or poor ground (G402).
Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect keyless access control unit connector B (32P).
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between keyless access control unit connector B (32P) terminals No. 7 and No. 8 and gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 29   and No. 30 respectively. Is there continuity? Replace the gauge control module  See: Instrument Panel, Gauges and Warning Indicators/Instrument Cluster / Carrier/Instrument Panel Control Module/Service and Repair. Repair an open or poor connection in the B-CAN wire between the gauge control module   and keyless access control unit.

dtcs.description: Gauge Control Module Lost Communication With Remote Slot Control Unit

steps.instruction: Clear the DTCs with the HDS.
Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF, ACC, and ON mode.
Wait for at least 6 seconds.

Check for DTCs with the HDS. Is DTC U1289 indicated? Go to step 5. Intermittent failure, the system is OK at this time. Check for loose or poor connections between the remote   slot control unit and the gauge control module.

From the BODY ELECTRICAL SYSTEM SELECT menu, select B-CAN CONTROL UNITS INFORMATION, and then select CHECK CONNECTED CONTROL UNITS. Is the remote slot control unit detected? Go to step 9. Go to step 6.
Measure the voltage between body ground and remote slot control unit 40P connector terminals No. 1 and No. 21 individually. Is there battery voltage? Go to step 7. Go to step 8.

Check the No. 15 (10 A) fuse in the under-hood fuse/relay box and the No. 9 (20 A) fuse in the driver's under-dash fuse/relay box. Are the fuses OK? Repair an open or high resistance in the No. 15(10 A)   and/or No. 9 (20 A)   fuse circuit. Replace the fuse(s). If the fuse blows again, repair a short to ground in the No. 15(10 A)   and/or No. 9(20 A)   fuse circuit.

Measure the voltage between body ground and remote slot control unit 40P connector terminals No. 20   and No. 40 individually. Is there less than 0.2 V? Go to step 9. Repair an open or high resistance in the ground wire or poor ground (G405).
Press the engine start/stop button to select the OFF mode.
Disconnect the remote slot control unit 40P connector.
Disconnect the gauge control module 32P connector.

Check for continuity between gauge control module 32P connector terminals No. 29   and No. 30   and remote slot control unit 40P connector terminals No. 23   and No. 3   respectively. Is there continuity? Replace the remote slot control unit  See: Accessories and Optional Equipment/Antitheft and Alarm Systems/Keyless Entry/Testing and Inspection/Component Tests and General Diagnostics/Keyless Access Remote Slot Test/Replacement. Repair an open or poor connection in the B-CAN wire between the remote   slot control unit and the gauge control module.
